edges.description: MFSD8 dysfunction is part of the NCL pathway that produces abnormal lysosomal storage material.
CLN7/MFSD8 loss-of-function model data link the upstream gene defect to autophagy failure.
Lysosomal storage pathology contributes to progressive neuronal dysfunction and loss.
Autophagy failure leads to accumulation of damaged neuronal mitochondria.
Damaged neuronal mitochondria generate elevated mitochondrial reactive oxygen species.
Elevated mitochondrial ROS drives PFKFB3 activation in CLN7 model neurons.
Aberrant neuronal PFKFB3 activation contributes to CLN7 pathogenesis and downstream neurodegeneration.
Progressive neuronal dysfunction contributes to epileptic seizures.
Progressive neuronal disease produces loss of acquired motor and cognitive function.
NCL7 neurodegeneration includes progressive retinal disease and loss of vision.
Progressive neurodegeneration causes cognitive decline in MFSD8-related disease.
Progressive neurodegeneration causes motor decline in MFSD8-related disease.
Progressive neurodegeneration can include behavioral impairment.
Progressive neuronal dysfunction produces myoclonus in classic CLN7 disease.
Retinal and neuronal degeneration causes progressive visual impairment.
Increased mitochondrial ROS directly reports the ROS node downstream of impaired autophagy and abnormal mitochondria.
Increased PFKFB3 reports activation of the glycolytic-enzyme node and can be used to read response to PFKFB3 inhibition in model systems.

nodes.description: MFSD8 gene replacement is a preclinical disease-targeted strategy. AAV9/MFSD8 improved lysosomal function in patient fibroblasts and extended lifespan in Mfsd8-deficient mice, but the cited evidence remains preclinical.

evidence.reference_title: AAV9/MFSD8 gene therapy is effective in preclinical models of neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis type 7 disease.
evidence.snippet: an in vitro study demonstrated that AAV2/MFSD8 dose dependently rescued lysosomal function in fibroblasts from a CLN7 patient.

evidence.reference_title: AAV9/MFSD8 gene therapy is effective in preclinical models of neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis type 7 disease.
evidence.snippet: A high dose of AAV9/MFSD8 at P7-P10 resulted in widespread MFSD8 mRNA expression, tendency of amelioration of subunit c of mitochondrial ATP synthase accumulation and glial fibrillary acidic protein immunoreactivity, normalization of impaired behaviors, doubled median life span, and extended normal body weight gain.

nodes.description: Mitochondrial ROS stabilizes and activates the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 model neurons, contributing to disease hallmarks.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Thus, aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in neurons may contribute to CLN7 pathogenesis

nodes.description: Impaired autophagy causes structurally and bioenergetically abnormal neuronal mitochondria to accumulate in CLN7 model neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: failure in autophagy causes accumulation of structurally and bioenergetically impaired neuronal mitochondria.

nodes.description: Behavioral impairment can accompany the progressive neurologic phenotype in severe childhood-onset MFSD8-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: presented in early childhood with intractable seizures, severe cognitive and motor decline, behavioral impairment and progressive retinal degeneration.

nodes.description: Progressive cognitive decline is part of the severe neurologic deterioration in MFSD8-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: presented in early childhood with intractable seizures, severe cognitive and motor decline, behavioral impairment and progressive retinal degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Cone-Rod Dystrophy and Progressive Visual Loss as the First Manifestation of Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis Type 7: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Two years later, she developed seizures and cognitive impairment, leading to a diagnosis of NCL7 resulting from a homozygote mutation in the MFSD8 gene.

nodes.description: Accumulated abnormal mitochondria in CLN7 model neurons produce elevated mitochondrial reactive oxygen species signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: In vivo genetic approach reveals elevated mitochondrial reactive oxygen species (mROS) in Cln7∆ex2 neurons that mediates glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 activation and contributes to CLN7 pathogenesis.

nodes.description: CLN7 loss-of-function model data show that autophagy failure is an early downstream cellular mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: failure in autophagy causes accumulation of structurally and bioenergetically impaired neuronal mitochondria.

nodes.description: CLN7 belongs to the neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses, which are defined by abnormal lysosomal accumulation of autofluorescent storage material. Reported MFSD8 cases show ultrastructural storage material in multiple patterns consistent with NCL.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: Neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (NCL) refers to a growing heterogeneous group of neurodegenerative disorders characterized by lysosomal accumulation of abnormal autofluorescent material.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: Ultrastructural studies of brain and rectal mucosa disclosed accumulation of storage material in various patterns including fingerprint, curvilinear, and granular osmiophilic deposits consistent with the diagnosis of NCL.

evidence.reference_title: The novel neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis gene MFSD8 encodes a putative lysosomal transporter.
evidence.snippet: We identified six different mutations in the MFSD8 gene (previously denoted "MGC33302"), which encodes a novel polytopic 518-amino acid membrane protein that belongs to the major facilitator superfamily of transporter proteins.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in MFSD8/CLN7 are a frequent cause of variant-late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: We identified nine children harboring 11 different mutations in MFSD8/CLN7.

evidence.reference_title: Case Report: Novel MFSD8 Variants in a Chinese Family With Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinoses 7.
evidence.snippet: we report a 4-year-old boy with CLN7 harboring compound heterozygous mutations in the MFSD8 gene, including one novel two-nucleotide deletion c.136_137delAT (p. M46Vfs*22) and one whole gene deletion of MFSD8 confirmed by Sanger sequencing, genomic quantitative PCR and CNV-seq.

nodes.description: MFSD8 encodes a polytopic major facilitator superfamily protein that localizes mainly to lysosomes. Pathogenic MFSD8 variants impair the CLN7 lysosomal membrane protein and initiate the NCL7 disease process.

evidence.reference_title: The novel neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis gene MFSD8 encodes a putative lysosomal transporter.
evidence.snippet: Like the majority of the previously identified NCL proteins, MFSD8 localizes mainly to the lysosomal compartment.

evidence.reference_title: AAV9/MFSD8 gene therapy is effective in preclinical models of neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis type 7 disease.
evidence.snippet: Neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis type 7 (CLN7) disease is a lysosomal storage disease caused by mutations in the facilitator superfamily domain containing 8 (MFSD8) gene, which encodes a membrane-bound lysosomal protein, MFSD8.

nodes.description: Milasen is an investigational n-of-1 splice-modulating antisense oligonucleotide developed for a single CLN7 patient whose MFSD8 intron 6 SVA retrotransposon insertion created a cryptic splice-acceptor site. It is designed to occlude that cryptic site and restore normal MFSD8 splicing. Milasen is customized to that patient's specific mutation and is not a generalizable CLN7 therapy; the evidence is a single expanded-access case.

evidence.reference_title: Patient-Customized Oligonucleotide Therapy for a Rare Genetic Disease.
evidence.snippet: We describe how molecular diagnosis of a rare, fatal neurodegenerative condition led to the rational design, testing, and manufacture of milasen, a splice-modulating antisense oligonucleotide drug tailored to a particular patient.

evidence.reference_title: Patient-Customized Oligonucleotide Therapy for a Rare Genetic Disease.
evidence.snippet: There were no serious adverse events, and treatment was associated with objective reduction in seizures (determined by electroencephalography and parental reporting).

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: In vivo genetic approach reveals elevated mitochondrial reactive oxygen species (mROS) in Cln7∆ex2 neurons that mediates glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 activation and contributes to CLN7 pathogenesis.

readouts.interpretation: Increased mitochondrial ROS directly reports the ROS node downstream of impaired autophagy and abnormal mitochondria.

nodes.description: Severe motor decline accompanies cognitive deterioration in classic MFSD8-related NCL7.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: presented in early childhood with intractable seizures, severe cognitive and motor decline, behavioral impairment and progressive retinal degeneration.

nodes.description: Myoclonus is included in the classic variant late-infantile NCL phenotype associated with CLN7/MFSD8.

evidence.reference_title: The novel neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis gene MFSD8 encodes a putative lysosomal transporter.
evidence.snippet: including age at onset from 2 to 7 years, epileptic seizures, psychomotor deterioration, myoclonus, loss of vision, and premature death.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Thus, aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in neurons may contribute to CLN7 pathogenesis

readouts.interpretation: Increased PFKFB3 reports activation of the glycolytic-enzyme node and can be used to read response to PFKFB3 inhibition in model systems.

nodes.description: PFKFB3 inhibition is an experimental downstream strategy in CLN7 model systems. AZ67 rectified key disease hallmarks in Cln7 mouse brain and patient-derived cells, but the evidence is preclinical.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Administration of the highly selective PFKFB3 inhibitor AZ67 in Cln7∆ex2 mouse brain in vivo and in CLN7 patients-derived cells rectifies key disease hallmarks.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant upregulation of the glycolytic enzyme PFKFB3 in CLN7 neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
evidence.snippet: Administration of the highly selective PFKFB3 inhibitor AZ67 in Cln7∆ex2 mouse brain in vivo and in CLN7 patients-derived cells rectifies key disease hallmarks.

nodes.description: NCL7 causes a progressive neurodegenerative phenotype that manifests as seizures, psychomotor deterioration, myoclonus, visual loss, cognitive decline, motor decline, and premature death.

evidence.reference_title: Update of the mutation spectrum and clinical correlations of over 360 mutations in eight genes that underlie the neuronal ceroid lipofuscinoses.
evidence.snippet: Clinical features include a variable age of onset, motor and mental decline, epilepsy, visual loss, and premature death.

nodes.description: Children with classic NCL7 lose motor and cognitive abilities during disease progression.

evidence.reference_title: The novel neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis gene MFSD8 encodes a putative lysosomal transporter.
evidence.snippet: including age at onset from 2 to 7 years, epileptic seizures, psychomotor deterioration, myoclonus, loss of vision, and premature death.

nodes.description: Retinal degeneration causes progressive visual loss and can be the initial presentation before seizures and cognitive impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Clinico-pathological manifestations of variant late infantile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (vLINCL) caused by a novel mutation in MFSD8 gene.
evidence.snippet: presented in early childhood with intractable seizures, severe cognitive and motor decline, behavioral impairment and progressive retinal degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Cone-Rod Dystrophy and Progressive Visual Loss as the First Manifestation of Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis Type 7: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: She originally presented with visual failure, which rapidly progressed to near total bilateral blindness.

nodes.description: Epileptic seizures are a core manifestation of classic CLN7 disease and can be intractable in severe cases.
